title: Schoenoplectiella lineolata
taxonomy: Schoenoplectiella lineolata is classified within the family Cyperaceae, commonly known as the sedge family. This family is part of the larger monocot group, which also includes other plants such as grasses and lilies. Schoenoplectiella lineolata is further classified within the order Poales and the class Liliopsida. At the kingdom level, it is classified as Plantae. Further classification includes the division Magnoliophyta and the phylum Angiospermophyta.

intro: Schoenoplectiella lineolata, also known as the river club-rush, is a type of aquatic plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of wounds, fever, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Schoenoplectiella lineolata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Schoenoplectiella lineolata is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the form of topical creams and ointments to treat skin conditions and wounds.
